content_markdown:
1

###  Application and Definitions

1.1

Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to:

  1. (1) a _large non-directive insurer_ ; and
  2. (2) a _Swiss general insurer_.

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

1.2

In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:

_management responsibilities map_

has the meaning given in [5.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#84381e5c35c245e9b262726761076a02 "5.1").

_prescribed responsibility_

  1. (1) for a _firm_ (other than a _Swiss general insurer_) means the responsibilities in [3.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#ec1719fd8d3d4a6781270c5e34dcd132 "3.1") and [3.2](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#22b18dc946cc4a26ac6fc7572b078886 "3.2");
  2. (2) for a _Swiss general insurer_ means the responsibilities set out in [3.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#ec1719fd8d3d4a6781270c5e34dcd132 "3.1") to the extent only that they are relevant to the activities of the _firm_ carried on from a _branch_ in the _UK_.

_Swiss general insurer prescribed responsibility_

means the responsibilities in [3A.2](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#0d0dbac25aa940728b2f30677663aabb "3A.2").

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2

###  Allocation of Responsibilities

2.1

A _firm_ (other than a _Swiss general insurer_) must allocate each of the _prescribed responsibilities_ set out in [3.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#ec1719fd8d3d4a6781270c5e34dcd132 "3.1") (other than [3.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#ec1719fd8d3d4a6781270c5e34dcd132 "3.1")(9) and (10)) to one or more _persons_ who, in relation to that _firm_ , are approved under section 59 of _FSMA_ by:

  1. (1) the _PRA_ to perform a _PRA senior management function_ ; or
  2. (2) in relation to _relevant senior management functions_ only, the _FCA_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.2

A _firm_ (other than a _Swiss general insurer_) must allocate each of the _prescribed responsibilities_ set out in [3.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#ec1719fd8d3d4a6781270c5e34dcd132 "3.1")(9) and (10) and the _prescribed responsibility_ set out in [3.2](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#22b18dc946cc4a26ac6fc7572b078886 "3.2"), if applicable to one or more _non-executive directors_ who perform:

  1. (1) a _PRA senior management function _set out in [Large Non-Solvency II Firms – Senior Insurance Management Functions 4](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-senior-management-functions#95b681bcdbf34038bbcc9c0a5aa36644 "Large Non-Solvency II Firms – Senior Insurance Management Functions 4"); or
  2. (2) an _FCA governing function_ at that _firm_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.3

A _Swiss general insurer_ must allocate each of the _prescribed responsibilities_ set out in [3.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#ec1719fd8d3d4a6781270c5e34dcd132 "3.1")(A1) to [3.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#ec1719fd8d3d4a6781270c5e34dcd132 "3.1")(1) and each of the _Swiss general insurer prescribed responsibilities_ to one or more _persons_ who, in relation to that _firm_, are approved under section 59 of _FSMA_ by:

  1. (1) the _PRA_ to perform a _PRA senior management function_ ; or
  2. (2) in relation to the _relevant senior management function_ only, the _FCA_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.4

A _firm_ must ensure that any responsibilities allocated to, and that form part of the responsibilities of, a _person_ who performs a _PRA senior management function_ in relation to the _firm_ are consistent with the scope of that _PRA senior management function_ and of any _prescribed responsibilities_ ,_FCA responsibilities_ and other responsibilities allocated to that _person_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

3

###  Prescribed Responsibilities

3.1

Each of the responsibilities set out in this rule is a _prescribed responsibility_ :

  1. (A1) responsibility for the _firm’s_ performance of its obligations under the _senior management regime_ (PR A);
  2. (A2) responsibility for the _firm’s_ performance of its obligations under the _certification regime_ (PR B);
  3. (A3) responsibility for compliance with the _firm’s_ obligations in relation to its _management responsibilities map_ (PR C);
  4. (1) responsibility for ensuring that the _firm_ has complied with its obligations in [Large Non-Solvency II Firms - Fitness and Propriety](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-fitness-and-propriety "Large Non-Solvency II Firms - Fitness and Propriety") to:
     1. (a) ensure that every _person_ who performs a _key function_ but does not also perform a _PRA senior management function_ or a _certification function_ is a fit and proper _person_ ; and
     2. (b) provide and obtain regulatory references (PR U);
  5. (2) responsibility for leading the development of the _firm’s_ culture by the _governing body_ as a whole (PR I);
  6. (3) responsibility for overseeing the adoption of the _firm’s_ culture in the day-to-day management of the _firm_ (PR H);
  7. (4) responsibility for the production and integrity of the _firm’s_ financial information and its regulatory reporting (PR Q);
  8. (5) responsibility for management of the allocation and maintenance of the _firm’s_ :
     1. (a) capital; and
     2. (b) liquidity (PR O);
  9. (6) responsibility for the development and maintenance of the _firm’s_ business model by the _governing body_ (PR T);
  10. (7) responsibility for leading the development and monitoring effective implementation of policies and procedures for the induction, training and professional development of all members of the _firm’s_ _governing body_ (PR F);
  11. (8) responsibility for monitoring effective implementation of policies and procedures for the induction, training and professional development of all of the _firm’s_ _key function holders_ (other than members of the _firm’s_ _governing body_) (PR G);
  12. (9) responsibility for oversight of the independence, autonomy and effectiveness of the _firm’s_ policies and procedures on whistleblowing including the procedures for protection of staff who raise concerns from detrimental treatment (PR N);
  13. (10) responsibility for overseeing the development and implementation of the _firm’s_ remuneration policies and practices (PR M); and
  14. (11) responsibility for the _firm’s_ performance of its obligations in respect of outsourced operational functions under [Non-Solvency II Firms – Governance 5](/pra-rules/non-solvency-ii-firms-governance#39968ab41b5243eca1c78add1a2c4d40 "Non-Solvency II Firms – Governance 5") (PR X).

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

3.2

The responsibility set out in rule 3.2(1) is a _prescribed responsibility_ in the circumstances specified:

  1. (1) if a _firm_ outsources its internal audit _function_ to an external third party service provider (that is, a service provider outside the _firm_ or the _firm’s_ _group_), responsibility for providing for an effective internal audit _function_ in accordance with [Non-Solvency II Firms – Governance 9.2](/pra-rules/non-solvency-ii-firms-governance#49ee326b4a004d2c814cbfadc8bb04e5 "Non-Solvency II Firms – Governance 9.2") and for overseeing the performance of the internal audit _function_ (PR J2).

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

3A

###  Prescribed Responsibilities: Swiss General Insurers

3A.1

This Chapter applies only to a _Swiss general insurer_ in respect of the activities of the _firm_ carried on from a _branch_ in the _UK_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

3A.2

Each of the responsibilities set out in this rule is a _Swiss general insurer prescribed responsibility_ :

  1. (1) responsibility for management of the _firm’s_ risk management processes in the _UK_ (PR AA);
  2. (2) responsibility for the _firm’s _compliance with the _UK_ _regulatory system_ applicable to the _firm_ (PR FF);
  3. (3) responsibility for the escalation of correspondence from the _PRA_ , _FCA_ and other regulators in respect of the _firm_ to each of the _governing body_ or the _management body_ of the _firm_ and, as appropriate, the _firm’s_ _parent undertaking_ and the ultimate _parent undertaking_ of the _firm’s_ _group_ (PR EE); and
  4. (4) responsibility for management of the _firm’s_ systems and controls in the _UK_ (PR BB).

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

4

###  Identification of Key Functions

4.1

A _firm_ must identify:

  1. (1) each of the _functions_ that the _firm _considers to be a _key function_ ; and
  2. (2) any such _key function_ that amounts to effectively running the _firm._

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

4.2

A _firm_ must keep its identification of _key functions_ pursuant to [4.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#db9088d2b4ab491998346b033a092eac "4.1") up-to-date.

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

4.3

A _firm_ must keep a record of its reasoning for the identification of _key functions_ pursuant to [4.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#db9088d2b4ab491998346b033a092eac "4.1").

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

5

###  Records

5.1

A _firm_ must have and maintain a _management responsibilities map_ , which is a clear and coherent document or series of documents with the following details:

  1. (1) a list of the _key functions_ identified by the _firm _in accordance with [4.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#db9088d2b4ab491998346b033a092eac "4.1") highlighting those that amount to effectively running the _firm_ ;
  2. (2) the names of the _persons_ who effectively run the _firm_ or who are responsible for other _key functions_ listed pursuant to [5.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#84381e5c35c245e9b262726761076a02 "5.1")(1);
  3. (3) for each _person_ named pursuant to [5.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#84381e5c35c245e9b262726761076a02 "5.1")(2), a summary of the significant responsibilities allocated to that _person_ (including, if applicable, any _prescribed responsibilities_ that have been allocated to that _person_ in accordance with [2](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#fef7e7dd057b484f80f982428e161428 "2"));
  4. (4) where any responsibilities covered by [5.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#84381e5c35c245e9b262726761076a02 "5.1")(3) are allocated to more than one _person_ , details of how those responsibilities are shared or divided between the _persons_ concerned;
  5. (5) reporting lines and lines of responsibility for each _person_ listed pursuant to [5.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#84381e5c35c245e9b262726761076a02 "5.1")(2);
  6. (6) where a _firm_ is a member of a _group_ :
     1. (a) how the _firm’s_ management and governance arrangements fit together with those of its _group_ and the extent to which the _firm’s_ management and governance arrangements are provided by or shared with other members of its _group_ ; and
     2. (b) for the _persons_ listed pursuant to [5.1](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#84381e5c35c245e9b262726761076a02 "5.1")(2), details of the reporting lines and the lines of responsibility (if any) to _persons_ who are employees or officers of other _group_ members or to committees or other bodies of the _group_ or of other _group _members;
  7. (7) matters reserved to the _governing body_ (including the terms of reference of its committees) including, in the case of a _Swiss general insurer_ , the equivalent body (or its committees) responsible for the management of the _Swiss general insurer’s_ business and activities in the _UK_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

5.2

A _firm_ must update the _management responsibilities map_ :

  1. (1) at least quarterly; and
  2. (2) in the event of a significant change to:
     1. (a) the _firm’s_ governance structure;
     2. (b) the significant responsibilities allocated to a _key function holder_ ; or
     3. (c) the reporting lines or lines of responsibility for a _key function holder_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

5.3

A _firm_ must, as soon as reasonably practicable, provide the following to the _PRA_ :

  1. (1) upon request by the _PRA_ , a copy of the _management responsibilities map_ ; and
  2. (2) in the event of an update pursuant to [5.2](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#2ecde02609b1437fb5107d108f58cf99 "5.2")(2), a copy of the relevant part of the _management responsibilities map_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

5.4

A _firm_ must keep an up-to-date record of the _statement of responsibilities_ of each _key function holder_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

5.5

A _statement of responsibilities form_ , where it is kept and maintained on behalf of a _key function holder_ , will satisfy the requirement in [5.4](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#fe0a70c7c890428aa354508b53c81825 "5.4").

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

5.6

The record in [5.4](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#fe0a70c7c890428aa354508b53c81825 "5.4"), and each updated version, must be signed by the _key function holder_ and an appropriate representative of the _firm_.

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

5.7

Where a _firm_ amends its _management responsibilities map_ to show changes in a _person’s_ responsibilities it must also ensure that:

  1. (1) the _person_ concerned is informed in writing of the changes; and
  2. (2) the record in [5.4](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#fe0a70c7c890428aa354508b53c81825 "5.4") is amended to show the changes.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

5.8

Each version of both the _management responsibilities map_ and the record in [5.4](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#fe0a70c7c890428aa354508b53c81825 "5.4") must be retained for a period of six years from the date on which it was superseded by a more up-to-date record, and must be provided to the _PRA_ on request.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

5.9

[Not currently used.]

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

5.10

A _firm_ must comply with [5.8](/pra-rules/large-non-solvency-ii-firms-allocation-of-responsibilities#850f3c8e0dde440ca3fc2c16c458161c "5.8") in relation to any record created in accordance with [SYSC 2.2.1R](/pra-rules/sysc-senior-management-arrangements-systems-and-controls-sourcebook-2#fe3cd3162ef64a679e6f0d4d3fdea317 "SYSC 2.2.1R") of the _PRA Handbook_ as at 31 December 2015.

*Effective: 07/03/2016*
content_html:
1

Application and Definitions

1.1

Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to:

  1. (1) a large non-directive insurer; and
  2. (2) a Swiss general insurer.
  • 07/03/2016

1.2

In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:

management responsibilities map

has the meaning given in 5.1.

prescribed responsibility

  1. (1) for a firm (other than a Swiss general insurer) means the responsibilities in 3.1 and 3.2;
  2. (2) for a Swiss general insurer means the responsibilities set out in 3.1 to the extent only that they are relevant to the activities of the firm carried on from a branch in the UK.

Swiss general insurer prescribed responsibility

means the responsibilities in 3A.2.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 1.2 before 10/12/2018

2

Allocation of Responsibilities

2.1

A firm (other than a Swiss general insurer) must allocate each of the prescribed responsibilities set out in 3.1 (other than 3.1(9) and (10)) to one or more persons who, in relation to that firm, are approved under section 59 of FSMA by:

  1. (1) the PRA to perform a PRA senior management function; or
  2. (2) in relation to relevant senior management functions only, the FCA.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.1 before 10/12/2018

2.2

A firm (other than a Swiss general insurer) must allocate each of the prescribed responsibilities set out in 3.1(9) and (10) and the prescribed responsibility set out in 3.2, if applicable to one or more non-executive directors who perform:

  1. (1) a PRA senior management function set out in Large Non-Solvency II Firms – Senior Insurance Management Functions 4; or
  2. (2) an FCA governing function at that firm.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.2 before 10/12/2018

2.3

A Swiss general insurer must allocate each of the prescribed responsibilities set out in 3.1(A1) to 3.1(1) and each of the Swiss general insurer prescribed responsibilities to one or more persons who, in relation to that firm, are approved under section 59 of FSMA by:

  1. (1) the PRA to perform a PRA senior management function; or
  2. (2) in relation to the relevant senior management function only, the FCA.
  • 10/12/2018

2.4

A firm must ensure that any responsibilities allocated to, and that form part of the responsibilities of, a person who performs a PRA senior management function in relation to the firm are consistent with the scope of that PRA senior management function and of any prescribed responsibilities, FCA responsibilities and other responsibilities allocated to that person.

  • 10/12/2018

3

Prescribed Responsibilities

3.1

Each of the responsibilities set out in this rule is a prescribed responsibility:

  1. (A1) responsibility for the firm’s performance of its obligations under the senior management regime (PR A);
  2. (A2) responsibility for the firm’s performance of its obligations under the certification regime (PR B);
  3. (A3) responsibility for compliance with the firm’s obligations in relation to its management responsibilities map (PR C);
  4. (1) responsibility for ensuring that the firm has complied with its obligations in Large Non-Solvency II Firms - Fitness and Propriety to:
    1. (a) ensure that every person who performs a key function but does not also perform a PRA senior management function or a certification function is a fit and proper person; and
    2. (b) provide and obtain regulatory references (PR U);
  5. (2) responsibility for leading the development of the firm’s culture by the governing body as a whole (PR I);
  6. (3) responsibility for overseeing the adoption of the firm’s culture in the day-to-day management of the firm (PR H);
  7. (4) responsibility for the production and integrity of the firm’s financial information and its regulatory reporting (PR Q);
  8. (5) responsibility for management of the allocation and maintenance of the firm’s:
    1. (a) capital; and
    2. (b) liquidity (PR O);
  9. (6) responsibility for the development and maintenance of the firm’s business model by the governing body (PR T);
  10. (7) responsibility for leading the development and monitoring effective implementation of policies and procedures for the induction, training and professional development of all members of the firm’s governing body (PR F);
  11. (8) responsibility for monitoring effective implementation of policies and procedures for the induction, training and professional development of all of the firm’s key function holders (other than members of the firm’s governing body) (PR G);
  12. (9) responsibility for oversight of the independence, autonomy and effectiveness of the firm’s policies and procedures on whistleblowing including the procedures for protection of staff who raise concerns from detrimental treatment (PR N);
  13. (10) responsibility for overseeing the development and implementation of the firm’s remuneration policies and practices (PR M); and
  14. (11) responsibility for the firm’s performance of its obligations in respect of outsourced operational functions under Non-Solvency II Firms – Governance 5 (PR X).
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 3.1 before 10/12/2018

3.2

The responsibility set out in rule 3.2(1) is a prescribed responsibility in the circumstances specified:

  1. (1) if a firm outsources its internal audit function to an external third party service provider (that is, a service provider outside the firm or the firm’s group), responsibility for providing for an effective internal audit function in accordance with Non-Solvency II Firms – Governance 9.2 and for overseeing the performance of the internal audit function (PR J2).
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 3.2 before 10/12/2018

3A

Prescribed Responsibilities: Swiss General Insurers

3A.1

This Chapter applies only to a Swiss general insurer in respect of the activities of the firm carried on from a branch in the UK.

  • 10/12/2018

3A.2

Each of the responsibilities set out in this rule is a Swiss general insurer prescribed responsibility:

  1. (1) responsibility for management of the firm’s risk management processes in the UK (PR AA);
  2. (2) responsibility for the firm’s compliance with the UK regulatory system applicable to the firm (PR FF);
  3. (3) responsibility for the escalation of correspondence from the PRA, FCA and other regulators in respect of the firm to each of the governing body or the management body of the firm and, as appropriate, the firm’s parent undertaking and the ultimate parent undertaking of the firm’s group (PR EE); and
  4. (4) responsibility for management of the firm’s systems and controls in the UK (PR BB).
  • 10/12/2018

4

Identification of Key Functions

4.1

A firm must identify:

  1. (1) each of the functions that the firm considers to be a key function; and
  2. (2) any such key function that amounts to effectively running the firm.
  • 07/03/2016

4.2

A firm must keep its identification of key functions pursuant to 4.1 up-to-date.

  • 07/03/2016

4.3

A firm must keep a record of its reasoning for the identification of key functions pursuant to 4.1.

  • 07/03/2016

5

Records

5.1

A firm must have and maintain a management responsibilities map, which is a clear and coherent document or series of documents with the following details:

  1. (1) a list of the key functions identified by the firm in accordance with 4.1 highlighting those that amount to effectively running the firm;
  2. (2) the names of the persons who effectively run the firm or who are responsible for other key functions listed pursuant to 5.1(1);
  3. (3) for each person named pursuant to 5.1(2), a summary of the significant responsibilities allocated to that person (including, if applicable, any prescribed responsibilities that have been allocated to that person in accordance with 2);
  4. (4) where any responsibilities covered by 5.1(3) are allocated to more than one person, details of how those responsibilities are shared or divided between the persons concerned;
  5. (5) reporting lines and lines of responsibility for each person listed pursuant to 5.1(2);
  6. (6) where a firm is a member of a group:
    1. (a) how the firm’s management and governance arrangements fit together with those of its group and the extent to which the firm’s management and governance arrangements are provided by or shared with other members of its group; and
    2. (b) for the persons listed pursuant to 5.1(2), details of the reporting lines and the lines of responsibility (if any) to persons who are employees or officers of other group members or to committees or other bodies of the group or of other group members;
  7. (7) matters reserved to the governing body (including the terms of reference of its committees) including, in the case of a Swiss general insurer, the equivalent body (or its committees) responsible for the management of the Swiss general insurer’s business and activities in the UK.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 5.1 before 10/12/2018

5.2

A firm must update the management responsibilities map:

  1. (1) at least quarterly; and
  2. (2) in the event of a significant change to:
    1. (a) the firm’s governance structure;
    2. (b) the significant responsibilities allocated to a key function holder; or
    3. (c) the reporting lines or lines of responsibility for a key function holder.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 5.2 before 10/12/2018

5.3

A firm must, as soon as reasonably practicable, provide the following to the PRA:

  1. (1) upon request by the PRA, a copy of the management responsibilities map; and
  2. (2) in the event of an update pursuant to 5.2(2), a copy of the relevant part of the management responsibilities map.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 5.3 before 10/12/2018

5.4

A firm must keep an up-to-date record of the statement of responsibilities of each key function holder.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 5.4 before 10/12/2018

5.5

A statement of responsibilities form, where it is kept and maintained on behalf of a key function holder, will satisfy the requirement in 5.4.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 5.5 before 10/12/2018

5.6

The record in 5.4, and each updated version, must be signed by the key function holder and an appropriate representative of the firm.

  • 07/03/2016

5.7

Where a firm amends its management responsibilities map to show changes in a person’s responsibilities it must also ensure that:

  1. (1) the person concerned is informed in writing of the changes; and
  2. (2) the record in 5.4 is amended to show the changes.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 5.7 before 10/12/2018

5.8

Each version of both the management responsibilities map and the record in 5.4 must be retained for a period of six years from the date on which it was superseded by a more up-to-date record, and must be provided to the PRA on request.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 5.8 before 10/12/2018

5.9

[Not currently used.]

  • 07/03/2016

5.10

A firm must comply with 5.8 in relation to any record created in accordance with SYSC 2.2.1R of the PRA Handbook as at 31 December 2015.

  • 07/03/2016
